task:
Open /home/user/Documents/students.xlsx in LibreOffice Calc. The workbook has two sheets:
- 'Roster' — Student ID (A), Name (B), Major (C); row 1 headers; rows 2-13 contain 12 students with IDs 1001-1012.
- 'Scores' — Student ID (A), Test 1 (B), Test 2 (C), Test 3 (D); row 1 headers; rows 2-13 contain the same 12 students' scores.

On the 'Scores' sheet:
- E1 = 'Student Name' (bold).
- E2:E13 = `=VLOOKUP(A2,Roster.$A$1:$B$13,2,0)` filled down (look up the student's name by Student ID).
- F1 = 'Average' (bold).
- F2:F13 = `=AVERAGE(B2:D2)` filled down.
- Sort the data range A1:F13 by column F (Average) in descending order, treating row 1 as headers so they stay in row 1 and the highest-scoring student is at the top.

Save the file.

verification.eval: result.get('formula') is not None and 'VLOOKUP' in result.get('formula','').upper()
verification.description: Scores E2 contains a VLOOKUP formula
Scores E2 = 'Irene Taylor' (highest avg student after sort)

verification.eval: result.get('formula') is not None and 'AVERAGE' in result.get('formula','').upper()
verification.description: Scores F2 contains an AVERAGE formula
Scores F2 value is approximately 94.67 (Irene Taylor's average after sort)
Column F is sorted descending from row 2
